Question Name: Is personal data accurate?
Question Description: Audit annually by legal team of data processing processes and procedures. See Privacy Notice, Audit Checklist for Compliance Procedure in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is personal data kept no longer than needed?
Question Description: Audit annually by legal team of data processing processes and procedures. See Privacy Notice, Audit Checklist for Compliance Procedure in Vrisk.

Question Name: Is the system measured to ensure it is processed securely in terms of integrity and confidentiality?
Question Description: Audit annually by legal team of data processing processes and procedures. See Privacy Notice, Audit Checklist for Compliance Procedure in Vrisk.

Question Name: Is there a data protection officer (DPO) in place that acts as the "controller" and is responsible for implementing appropriate privacy controls (including policies and codes of conduct) considering the risks, rights and other requirements of the GDPR?
Question Description: A DPO is responsible to implement policies and privacy controls that include a privacy impact assessment to measure risk and effectiveness of privacy controls and other requirements. See Rationale for a DPO, Data Protection Officer (DPO) Job Responsibilities, and  Data Protection Officer (DPO) Job Description in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place for privacy by design?
Question Description: The software development lifecycle policy and procedures should include privacy requirements that are embedded in the design and not bolted on afterwards. The DPO should be a part of this process.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place where organizations are jointly responsible for determining and fulfilling privacy requirements collaboratively that clarifies their respective roles and responsibilities?
Question Description: The DPO should have processes and procedures set up to collaborate with teams across the organization including compliance, security, business owners, data stewards, governance teams and development teams.

Question Name: For organizations outside Europe, is there a formal privacy representative? And inside Europe if they meet certain conditions (e.g. they routinely supply goods and services to, or monitor, Europeans)?
Question Description: Privacy is now one of the top 3 business concerns.  A formal privacy representative should be appointed for organizations outside of the EU.

Question Name: If an organization uses one or more third parties to process personal info ('processors'),  can it ensure they too are compliant with GDPR?
Question Description: Vendor relationship management processes and procedures must ensure that third parties that process personal information are complaint with the GDPR.  This can also be a role of the DPO if there is no vendor management team.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to ensure processors only process personal info in accordance with instructions from the controller and applicable laws?
Question Description: The DPO must have a process to review the data processors' mechanisms to ensure processing is lawful and correct.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place for controllers to maintain documentation concerning privacy e.g. the purposes for which personal info is gathered and processed, 'categories' of data subjects and personal data etc.?
Question Description: Data classification processes and procedures should be in place to identify which systems and technologies process categories of data.  The DPO should have processes that are documented and which relate to purposes for which personal info is gathered and processed. ValuRisQ allows for categorization of data processed in system and stored in databases.  See Information Classification Procedure in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a data protection officer position?
Question Description: See the rationale for a DPO in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is the DPO supported by the organization and engaged in privacy matters?
Question Description: See Data Protection Officer (DPO) Job Responsibilities in Vrisk.

Question Name: Does the DPO offer advice on privacy matters, monitor compliance, liaise with the authorities, act as a contact point, address privacy risks etc.?
Question Description: See the Data Protection Officer (DPO) Job Responsibilities in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to align the GDPR assessment process with other codes of conduct, regulations and guidelines?
Question Description: Utilize the ValuRisQ Control Mapping Framework.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to demonstrate the GDPR assessment process with other codes of conduct, regulations and guidelines?
Question Description: Utilize the VRisk Control Mapping Framework.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism for international transfers and processing of personal info to be compliant with GDPR?
Question Description: See transfers of personal data to 3rd countries or international organizations Procedure in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to identify which systems transfer GDPR data and to which locations that are identified as adequate?
Question Description: VRisk shows where data is transferred and can tag entities with adequate or inadequate privacy arrangements and systems that use them.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to identify which systems transfer GDPR data and to which locations that are identified as not adequate? Is there a mechanism in place for the organization to verify the implementation and adequacy of privacy controls before transferring personal data to such countries, and subsequently e.g. suitable contractual clauses and compliance activities?
Question Description: VRisk shows where data is transferred and can tag entities with adequate or inadequate privacy arrangements and systems that use them.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place for national authorities to approve legally-binding privacy rules permitting transfers to non-approved countries?
Question Description: See List of Authorities and Key Suppliers and Contact with Authorities Work Instruction in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to measure the risk of data transfers that are to non-approved countries?
Question Description: Requires a Data Loss Prevention system.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to communicate with international authorities on privacy issues?
Question Description: See List of Authorities and Key Suppliers and Contact with Authorities Work Instruction in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism for supervisory authorities to deal with privacy complaints?
Question Description: For Supervisory Authorities and not organizations.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism for individuals damaged by infringements of GDPR to apply for compensation?
Question Description: For Supervisory Authorities and not organizations.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to pay for administrative GDPR fines?
Question Description: For Supervisory Authorities and not organizations.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to understand other penalties that may be imposed?
Question Description: For Supervisory Authorities and not organizations.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to balance privacy/data protection rights against freedom of expression, journalism, academic research etc. through suitable laws?
Question Description: For Supervisory Authorities and not organizations.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to disclose personal data if the documents are formally required to be disclosed under 'freedom of information' - type laws?
Question Description: Use the Operational Procedure in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to provide further privacy controls for national ID numbers?
Question Description: Use the Operational Procedure in ValuRisQ.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism to show how employment laws intersect with GDPR and privacy?
Question Description: Use the ValuRisQ Framework Mapping.

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place for pseudonymization and data minimization where personal data is to be archived e.g. for research and statistical purposes?
Question Description: Pseudonymization Policy and Procedure

Question Name: Is there a mechanism in place to allow for additional nation laws concerning employment and secrecy laws?
Question Description: Use the ValuRisQ Framework Mapping and Regulations.

Question Name: Are there mechanism in place to ensure that organizations implement, operate and maintain appropriate technical and organizational security measures for personal info, addressing the information risks?
Question Description: Utilize the ValuRisQ Privacy Impact Assessment and Security Assessment Module.
